title: Found.
content:
ONCE through the forest

    Alone I went;
    To seek for nothing

    My thoughts were bent.

    I saw i' the shadow

    A flower stand there
    As stars it glisten'd,

    As eyes 'twas fair.

    I sought to pluck it, 

    It gently said:
    "Shall I be gather'd

    Only to fade?"

    With all its roots

    I dug it with care,
    And took it home

    To my garden fair.

    In silent corner

    Soon it was set;
    There grows it ever,

    There blooms it yet.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, titled "Found.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
